title: Assessment of the in vitro trypanocidal activity
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"2sv0i","text":"The aim of this experiment is to assess the potential of a plant extract to fight off Trypanosoma species, including Trypanosoma brucei brucei, Trypanosoma evansi, etc., by testing it at concentrations ranging from 006.25 to 400.00 mg/mL. The results obtained indicate that the plant extract may be a useful tool in the management of trypanosomiasis, but further studies are required to confirm its effectiveness.","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":117,"length":26},{"style":"italic","offset":144,"length":18}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"3l4vq","text":"The experimental animals need to be handled in accordance with Good Laboratory Practices and Guidelines for Laboratory Animal Facilities and the recommendations of the Committee on Animal Use and Care","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"ctnu9","text":"The experimental animals should be screened for any ailment at the commencement of the experiment using the laboratory’s recommended procedure and cleared of any ailment.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"19sos","text":"Handling the experimental animals may require training to reduce the risk of injury to the animal and the researcher","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: The infected rat to undergo euthanasia must have attained a blood parasitemia of log 8.4 or higher Trypanosoma brucei: a rapid "matching" method for estimating the host's parasitemia. Experimental Parasitology. .
Euthanatized animal’s blood was dissolved in heparin ( 1 mL of heparin per 10 mL of blood) and mixed with glucose ( 0.1 g of glucose per 10 mL of blood).
Then, aseptically, a clean micropipette transfers the blood ( 50 µL ) to a clean, sterile microtiter plate into multiple wells.
To the two wells containing blood, the same volume (i.e., 50 µL ) of the highest drug concentration was added, respectively.
The negative control blood was mixed with the dilutant used to prepare the various concentrations (i.e., dextrose-saline or water).
The positive tests were also performed with the standard recommended concentrationsof a commercially available trypanocide drug.
Step 4 was repeated for the other different concentrations available.

To produce a stock Sample solution of 100 mg/mL for the extract, weigh 1 g ( 1000 mg ) of the extract, which was solubilized in 1 mL of dimethylsulfoxide (DMSO) solution and made up to 9 mL in dextrose saline/normal saline.
Serial dilutions were made for the remaining concentration of 50 mg/mL , 25 mg/mL , 12.5 mg/mL and 6.25 mg/mL of the extracts were made in Dextrose saline (freshly prepared).

At 00:05:00 5-minute interval, a drop of blood is taken from each well and smeared on a clean glass slide. then observe under a microscope at 400x for signs of motility unique to Trypanosoma spp. Trypanosoma brucei: A rapid “matching” method for estimating the host's parasitemia. Experimental Parasitology. Options for field diagnosis of human african trypanosomiasis.. Clinical Microbiology Reviews.
The above step is repeated with each well. Till motility stopped in all wells
